title: Boat Rental in Fort Lauderdale: Costs, Tips and What to Know in 2026
html:
Fort Lauderdale, often called the Venice of America, offers over 300 miles of navigable waterways. A typical half-day rental from Bahia Mar Yachting Center costs between 350 and 700 USD. Find your perfect vessel on BoatHire24, a worldwide marketplace connecting renters with boat owners.

Where to set off in Fort Lauderdale

You can launch from several well-equipped marinas: Bahia Mar Yachting Center, Lauderdale Marina, and Harbour Towne Marina are popular choices. The Intracoastal Waterway runs through the city, giving direct access to Lake Sylvia, Port Everglades, and the Atlantic Ocean. For a day trip, head to Sands Harbour or the nearby town of Dania Beach. Many rentals also include mooring at Las Olas Marina for a lunch stop.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Fort Lauderdale?

Prices vary by boat type and duration, typically from 200 USD for a small skiff to 2,500 USD for a crewed yacht per day. To get the best deal, browse the listings on boat rental Fort Lauderdale where you can compare prices and boats side by side.

  • Shared sightseeing tour: from 75 USD per person for a two-hour trip
  • Small motorboat (e.g. 19-foot bowrider) half-day: 300, 450 USD
  • Catamaran full-day: 900, 1,500 USD for up to 12 guests
  • Crewed yacht (e.g. 45-foot Sunseeker) full-day: 2,000, 2,500 USD

Which boat to rent

Choose a boat based on your group size and planned activities; here are four popular options available through BoatHire24.

  1. Bowrider (19-24 ft): Ideal for cruising the Intracoastal and beach hopping, seats 6, 8 guests, from 350 USD half-day.
  2. Centre console (24-28 ft): Perfect for fishing or watersports, holds up to 10, from 500 USD half-day.
  3. Catamaran (35-45 ft): Great for parties and snorkelling trips, accommodates 12, 15, from 1,000 USD full-day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (40-50 ft): All-inclusive luxury for up to 10, with skipper and fuel, from 2,000 USD full-day.
  5. Pontoon boat: Stable and easy, ideal for families, 8, 12 guests, from 250 USD half-day.

For a spirited day on the water, consider the QuickSilver Activ 605 Open - 150 HP listing, a 20-footer that can reach 35 knots and accommodates up to six people.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Fort Lauderdale?

No boating licence is required for most rentals if you stay within sight of the marina, but operators may ask for a brief checkout. The legal age to operate a boat in Florida is 14 with a Boater Safety Education Card for those born after 1988. BoatHire24 operators confirm exact requirements at check-in, so you can turn up without worry.

Local tip: Reserve your boat early in the morning, especially during high season (December to April), when the sea breeze known as the 'Northerly' can kick up waves later in the day.

At a glance

  • Price band: 200, 2,500 USD per day
  • Peak months: December through April
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours (half-day) or 8 hours (full-day)
  • Capacity: from 6 to 30 guests, depending on the vessel
  • Currency: USD

Questions about boat rental in Fort Lauderdale

Can I rent a boat without a captain?

Yes, many rentals are bareboat and you drive yourself; a captain is optional at extra cost.

What is the cancellation policy?

Policies vary by owner but typically allow free cancellation up to 48 hours before departure.

Are pets allowed on board?

Some listings welcome well-behaved pets; check the individual boat's rules before booking.

What happens if the weather is bad?

If winds exceed 20 knots or lightning is forecast, you can reschedule at no charge or receive a full refund.

Whether you are a first-time renter or an experienced skipper, booking through BoatHire24 gives you access to verified vessels in Fort Lauderdale. If you own a boat, consider listing it on BoatHire24 to reach thousands of travellers. You can also list your boat if you have one.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Fort Lauderdale and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
